description: A batch of focused layout, alignment, spacing, and visual polish improvements to the Chrome extension side panel. Each story targets one specific visual issue to bring the neobrutalist UI to a higher level of fit-and-finish.

userStories.title: Fix footer border weight inconsistency
userStories.description: As a user, I want the footer border to match the header border weight so the panel feels visually balanced.
acceptanceCriteria: Footer uses border-t-2 instead of border-t
Header and footer borders are both 2px, matching the neobrutalist design system
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/Footer.tsx:2. Change `border-t` to `border-t-2` in the footer className. The Header.tsx already uses `border-b-2` — this just makes them consistent.

userStories.title: Fix accordion content top/bottom padding asymmetry
userStories.description: As a user, I want consistent spacing above and below tools in an expanded plugin card so the layout feels balanced.
acceptanceCriteria: The Accordion.Content inner wrapper in retro/Accordion.tsx no longer has pb-1
The inner wrapper uses className 'px-0 pt-0 pb-0' or just 'px-0' (no vertical padding)
Tool rows' own py-2 provides all vertical spacing within the content area
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Accordion.tsx:50. The inner div has className `cn('px-0 pt-0 pb-1', className)`. Change `pb-1` to `pb-0`. The tool rows already have `py-2` and `last:border-b-0`, so the extra 4px bottom padding makes the bottom spacier than the top. Removing it creates symmetry.

userStories.title: Indent tool rows for parent-child visual hierarchy
userStories.description: As a user, I want tool rows visually indented under their parent plugin name so the hierarchy is immediately clear.
acceptanceCriteria: Tool rows in ToolRow.tsx use pl-5 instead of px-3 (left padding increases from 12px to 20px)
Tool display names visually sit indented relative to the plugin display name above
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ToolRow.tsx:24. Currently the tool row className includes `px-3`. Change to `pl-5 pr-3`. This indents the tool icon+name rightward, creating a clear parent-child relationship with the plugin header row above (which uses `px-3` with a 32px icon). The goal: tool names should feel nested under their plugin, not at the same hierarchy level. Also update the filter count text in PluginCard.tsx:150 from `px-3` to `pl-5 pr-3` to match the tool row indentation.

userStories.title: Move TabStateHint inside accordion content area
userStories.description: As a user, I want the tab state hint (e.g., 'Open slack.com in your browser') to appear inside the expanded content area, not floating between the header and content.
acceptanceCriteria: TabStateHint renders inside <Accordion.Content> as the first element, before tool rows
TabStateHint no longer has ml-3 and pl-10 magic indentation — uses pl-5 pr-3 matching tool rows
TabStateHint is only visible when the accordion is open (inherits accordion expand/collapse behavior)
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx. Currently the <TabStateHint> at line 146 sits between the toggle error alert and the <Accordion.Content>. Move it inside <Accordion.Content> as the first child (before the filter count and tool rows). Update the TabStateHint component (lines 24-38) to use `pl-5 pr-3 py-2 text-[11px]` instead of `ml-3 pr-3 pb-2 pl-10 text-[11px]` — this matches the tool row left indentation from US-003. Remove the `ml-3` and `pl-10` magic numbers entirely.

userStories.title: Add top padding to filter count text
userStories.description: As a user, I want the filter count text ('3 of 5 tools') to have breathing room from the top border of the accordion content.
acceptanceCriteria: Filter count text div in PluginCard.tsx has pt-2 added
There is 8px of space between the border-t of accordion content and the filter count text
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx:150. The filter count div currently has className `text-muted-foreground mb-1 px-3 text-xs`. Add `pt-2` to give breathing room from the border-t of the accordion content. Also update px-3 to pl-5 pr-3 to match tool row indentation from US-003. Final className: `text-muted-foreground mb-1 pl-5 pr-3 pt-2 text-xs`.

userStories.title: Constrain empty state separator width
userStories.description: As a user, I want the accent separator in empty states to be a punchy accent bar, not a full-width divider.
acceptanceCriteria: EmptySeparator in retro/Empty.tsx uses w-16 instead of w-full
The purple separator bar is 64px wide, centered within the empty state card
Both DisconnectedState and EmptyState show the constrained separator
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Empty.tsx:39. The EmptySeparator className is `bg-primary h-1 w-full`. Change `w-full` to `w-16`. The parent EmptyContent uses `flex flex-col items-center` so the separator will auto-center. This creates a more intentional design accent rather than a heavy horizontal rule.

userStories.title: Add left accent border on open accordion cards
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clear visual indicator showing which plugin card is currently expanded.
acceptanceCriteria: AccordionItem in retro/Accordion.tsx includes data-[state=open]:border-l-primary in its className
When a plugin card is expanded, its left border turns purple (the primary color)
When collapsed, the left border returns to the normal border color
The transition feels smooth (transition-all already exists on the component)
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Accordion.tsx:14-16. The AccordionItem className currently is: `bg-background text-foreground overflow-hidden rounded border-2 shadow-md transition-all hover:shadow-sm data-[state=open]:shadow-sm`. Add `data-[state=open]:border-l-primary` to the string. The `border-2` already applies a 2px border on all sides, so the left border color override will work directly. The `transition-all` already on the element will animate the color change.

userStories.title: Tone down Connected badge prominence
userStories.description: As a user, I want the 'Connected' status to be ambient information, not the loudest element in the header.
acceptanceCriteria: Connected badge in Header.tsx uses variant='default' (muted background) instead of variant='surface' (solid purple)
Disconnected badge remains loud with destructive styling (unchanged)
The connected state reads as calm/ambient while disconnected reads as urgent
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/Header.tsx:11. Change `variant="surface"` to `variant="default"`. The Badge component's 'default' variant uses `bg-muted text-muted-foreground` which is a soft lavender background — much less visually dominant than the solid purple 'surface' variant. The Disconnected badge (line 15) stays as-is with its destructive red.

userStories.title: Standardize header and plugin trigger vertical padding
userStories.description: As a user, I want consistent vertical rhythm across the header and plugin card headers.
acceptanceCriteria: Header in Header.tsx uses py-2.5 (unchanged, already correct)
Plugin card trigger in PluginCard.tsx uses py-2 instead of py-2.5
The plugin header rows feel slightly more compact, matching the density of tool rows (which also use py-2)
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx:109. The AccordionPrimitive.Trigger className includes `py-2.5`. Change to `py-2`. This makes plugin triggers match tool row density (also py-2). The header stays at py-2.5 — it's the app chrome, so slightly more padding is appropriate. The plugin cards are content rows and should be denser.
